title: Megastigma conzattii

This family consists of approximately 160 genera, including those that produce fruits like oranges and lemons. Megastigma conzattii is also classified as a member of the order Sapindales, which is a group of flowering plants that includes other families like Anacardiaceae and Burseraceae. This classification is based on the plant's morphological and molecular characteristics, which have been studied and analyzed by botanists.

intro: Megastigma conzattii, also known as Conzatti's Megastigma, is a plant species in the family Rutaceae, commonly found in Mexico and Central America. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate conditions such as arthritis and wound infections. The bioactive constituents of Megastigma conzattii include limonoids, flavonoids, and coumarins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
